UsageCost: KWh:8CZK for ČEZ registered users;10CZK for non-registered users;11.5CZK for E.On users.

Connections.Comments: L-N voltage mentioned(L-L voltage is 400V). KWh:8CZK for ČEZ registered users;10CZK for non-registered users;11.5CZK for E.On users.

GeneralComments: 4 dedicated parking bays @ public parking lot,2 charging stations,2 sockets each(4 sockets total). Great as C+R(Charge+Ride):U can dock Your vehicle here for charging & have a railroad trip in meantime,or use it to visit Pardubice by train,if U dislike driving in dense traffic in a big city,also good for combined daily commuting(car+train).But bad for staying in place,while charging,as almost no restaurants nor pubs nearby accept credit cards,the nearest restaurant accepting credit cards is more than 1Km away & has rude stuff(all restaurants here has incredibly rude stuff),thus also U have a huge problem to use a toilet(in Czechia only paying customers can use toilets in restaurants/pubs),thus Your best option is to go by train to Pardubice & use the restaurant there on railroad station & then return again by train & Your vehicle will be just recharged.So the nearest good option to have a warm food with acceptable quality,be able to pay it by card & have a clean restrooms is in Pardubice,hl.n..
